title: Robustly Crispy Kenyan Meat Skewers
description: A peppery and crispy Kenyan dish featuring caramelized sugar sauce, this recipe is perfect for those looking for a flavorful and energizing meal. The agemono-deep-fry method ensures a crunchy exterior and a juicy interior.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a copper pot over medium-high heat to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Thread the beef pieces onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Mix the onion, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, black pepper, and salt in a bowl.
Add the sugar to the bowl and mix until well combined.
Dip each skewer into the sugar mixture, coating the beef evenly.
Deep-fry the skewers in the copper pot for 5-7 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
Remove the skewers from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the skewers hot with a side of caramelized sugar sauce for dipping.
To make the caramelized sugar sauce, melt 2 tablespoons of sugar in a saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly.
